title: YouTube hid the new DMs... Here is how to find them!
excerpt: Explains where YouTube hid its new mobile DMs, how invite links work and why the rollout feels limited.
contentHtml:
Transcript

YouTube secretly brought back direct messages, but don't get your hopes up. >> Oh, Here we go again. It is an absolute UX nightmare. No threads, no swipe to reply, no GIFs, and definitely no group chats. Oh, and it's strictly mobile only right now. YouTube hid the whole thing completely out of sight in your notifications, but if you still want to put yourself through this, here's a quick tutorial.

DMs are currently rolling out in European countries and require an 18+ account. Open the app, go to your inbox, and tap the chat icon at the top. But to actually message someone, you have to generate an invite link and send it via a third-party platform like WhatsApp or Discord. Yeah, seriously. Let me know in the comments, are you giving this a chance, or will this feature be dead again in 6 months?
